summary.intent: Fix a race condition (P1) in ChatService::compact where broadcasts fired before the session store was updated, and address a payload shape inconsistency (P2) between two auto-compact code paths.
summary.outcome: Reordered replace_history/session_metadata.touch to precede broadcasts in ChatService::compact, and added structural markers (compactBroadcastPath: 'inner'/'wrapper') to distinguish the two auto-compact done payloads. All tests passed; changes committed and pushed, triggering round 5.

repo: ChatService::compact previously broadcast chat.compact done and notified channels before calling session_store.replace_history, creating a window where concurrent send() RPCs could read stale history.

There are two distinct auto-compact code paths — one through send() and one through run_with_tools — that emit auto_compact done events with inconsistent payloads (send() omits mode/token metadata present in run_with_tools).
The review process uses multi-round automated analysis; this session addressed findings from round 4 and then triggered round 5.

code.finding: replace_history and session_metadata.touch must precede any broadcasts or channel notifications in ChatService::compact to prevent concurrent readers from seeing stale history.
send()'s auto_compact done payload was missing mode/token metadata; compactBroadcastPath: 'inner' marker added so hook consumers can detect which path fired.
run_with_tools auto_compact done tagged with compactBroadcastPath: 'wrapper' to distinguish it from the send() path.

workflow: The iterative round-based review loop (poll → read findings → fix → commit → trigger next round) is the established workflow for this branch.
P1 race conditions should be fixed before addressing P2 shape inconsistencies.

open_items: Round 5 was triggered but its results are not yet available; further findings may require additional fixes.

The P2 fix uses structural markers and comments as a workaround rather than unifying the two auto-compact payload shapes — the real fix would be having ChatService::compact return metadata so send() can merge it into its broadcast.
